subject: [PATCH RFC 00/32] mm/mglru: MGLRU-FG and refault distance support

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer pointed out that rearranging the flags for MGLRU-FG and removing single-bit operations would simplify the code, but this would require reordering the patches so that the flag unification work comes first.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi,

That's not how it works, though. Without the MGLRU-FG, it takes four
bits for tracking the tier and these two flag bits have special
meanings. After FG, only three bits are needed and these two original
flag bits are just the lower two bits of a three-bit counter now. So,
there is an advantage to rearranging them together and removing the
single-bit (flag) operations to always use them as a whole. Before
that, that conversion will actual make thing more ugly.

BTW will there be any feature change with your splitting plan? If
there is no feature change, that should be easy to review.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch series introduces MGLRU-FG (Multi-Granularity Least Recently Used - Fault Generation) and refault distance support, which aims to improve the performance of page faults in Linux by allowing for more efficient handling of page replacements. The patches are designed to work with a new flag format that unifies various flags used in the kernel. However, there is a concern about the order of review and submission, as some developers think it would be better to first land the flag format changes and then submit the MGLRU-FG patches separately. This would allow for concurrent review and potentially speed up the process.
